name: azure-ai-projects-py
description: Build AI applications using the Azure AI Projects Python SDK (azure-ai-projects). Use when working with Foundry project clients, creating versioned agents with PromptAgentDefinition, running evaluations, managing connections/deployments/datasets/indexes, or using OpenAI-compatible clients. This is the high-level Foundry SDK - for low-level agent operations, use azure-ai-agents-python skill.
capabilities: Create and manage AI agents with the AIProjectClient
Configure agents with tools like CodeInterpreter, FileSearch, and BingGrounding
Manage conversation threads and message flows
Set up versioned agents using PromptAgentDefinition
Connect to external services through connections API
Run evaluations using built-in and custom evaluators
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All 74 static findings are false positives. The file is a markdown documentation file (SKILL.md) containing code examples for the Azure AI Projects Python SDK. The 'external_commands' detections are markdown code fences, not shell execution. The 'network' detection is a placeholder URL template in documentation. The 'env_access' detections are documentation examples showing SDK configuration. No actual executable code or malicious patterns exist. This is a legitimate documentation skill for Microsoft's Azure Foundry SDK.
valueStatement: Build AI applications on Microsoft Foundry using the Azure AI Projects Python SDK. This skill provides comprehensive documentation for creating agents, managing threads, configuring tools, and running evaluations.
userTitle: Build Azure AI Apps with Foundry SDK
limitations: This is a documentation skill - it provides reference documentation, not code execution
Requires Azure subscription and Foundry project setup
Authentication uses DefaultAzureCredential which requires proper Azure identity configuration
Some features require specific Azure AI Foundry plan tiers

useCases.targetUser: Developers building AI applications on Microsoft Azure Foundry
useCases.title: Build AI Agents in Microsoft Foundry
useCases.description: Create intelligent agents that can execute Python code, search files, and call external APIs using the Azure AI Projects SDK.

useCases.targetUser: AI engineers configuring agent capabilities in Azure
useCases.title: Configure Agent Tools and Capabilities
useCases.description: Add capabilities like code interpretation, file search, Bing grounding, and custom function calling to your agents.

useCases.targetUser: ML engineers performing AI evaluation and testing
useCases.title: Run Evaluations on Agent Outputs
useCases.description: Use built-in evaluators or create custom evaluators to test and measure agent quality and adherence to requirements.

promptTemplates.title: Create Basic Agent
promptTemplates.prompt: Create an Azure AI Projects agent named 'my-agent' using the gpt-4o-mini model with the instruction 'You are a helpful assistant.' Use the AIProjectClient with DefaultAzureCredential.
promptTemplates.scenario: Setting up a simple agent with basic configuration

promptTemplates.title: Agent with Code Interpreter
promptTemplates.prompt: Create an agent with CodeInterpreterTool and FileSearchTool enabled. The agent should be able to execute Python code and search through uploaded documents.
promptTemplates.scenario: Adding tool capabilities to an agent

promptTemplates.title: Conversation Thread Flow
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to create a thread, add a user message, create and process a run, and retrieve the assistant response using the Azure AI Projects SDK.

promptTemplates.title: Versioned Agent for Production
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a versioned agent using PromptAgentDefinition with version label 'v1.0'. Include instructions for a customer support specialist and show how to deploy it.

bestPractices: Use context managers (async with) for the async AIProjectClient to ensure proper resource cleanup
Create versioned agents with PromptAgentDefinition for production deployments to maintain stable APIs
Clean up agents with client.agents.delete_agent(agent.id) when no longer needed to avoid resource leaks
antiPatterns: Do not hardcode credentials in code - always use DefaultAzureCredential or environment variables
Do not use the low-level AgentsClient when you need Foundry-specific features like connections and deployments
Avoid creating many agents without cleanup - always delete agents when done

faq.question: What is the difference between AIProjectClient and AgentsClient?
faq.answer: AIProjectClient is a high-level client for full Foundry integration with connections, deployments, datasets, and versioning. AgentsClient is a low-level client for standalone agent applications without Foundry features.

faq.question: How do I authenticate to Azure AI Projects?
faq.answer: Use DefaultAzureCredential which automatically handles authentication through Azure AD, managed identities, or environment variables.

faq.question: Can I use OpenAI API directly with this SDK?
faq.answer: Yes, use client.get_openai_client() to get an OpenAI-compatible client for standard OpenAI API calls.

faq.question: What tools can I add to my agents?
faq.answer: You can add CodeInterpreterTool, FileSearchTool, BingGroundingTool, AzureAISearchTool, FunctionTool, OpenApiTool, McpTool, MemorySearchTool, and SharepointGroundingTool.

faq.question: How do I create versioned agents?
faq.answer: Use client.agents.create_version() with a PromptAgentDefinition to create versioned agents with stable APIs for production.

faq.question: Does this support async operations?
faq.answer: Yes, use AIProjectClient from azure.ai.projects.aio for async operations with async/await patterns.
